story.iWant: Docker Compose ile tüm servisleri başlatabilmek
story.soThat: local environment tek komutla ayağa kalksın
story.tasks: 1. Create docker-compose.yml file 2. Configure app service (build context, port mapping, volume mount, environment, depends_on) 3. Configure PostgreSQL service (image, environment, volume, port, health check) 4. Configure MongoDB service (image, environment, volume, port) 5. Configure Redis service (image, volume, port, command) 6. Add NPM scripts for Docker Compose 7. Test Docker Compose workflow

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. docker/docker-compose.yml dosyası oluşturulmuş 2. Services tanımlı: app (NestJS), postgres (PostgreSQL 15), mongodb (MongoDB 6), redis (Redis 7) 3. App service: depends_on postgres/mongodb, source code volume mount, environment variables, port 3000:3000 4. Postgres service: environment variables, named volume postgres_data, port 5432:5432, pg_isready health check 5. MongoDB service: environment variables, named volume mongo_data, port 27017:27017 6. Redis service: named volume redis_data, port 6379:6379 7. Commands: docker-compose up -d, docker-compose logs -f app, docker-compose down

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification: Development Environment
doc.section: APIs and Interfaces → Docker Compose Service Interfaces
doc.snippet: Epic 10, Docker tabanlı, tutarlı ve hızlı kurulabilen bir local development environment oluşturmayı hedefler. Docker Compose orchestration (app + postgres + mongodb + redis) ile tek komutla tüm servisleri başlatabilme.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: docker-compose.yml: Multi-service orchestration. App service build context use docker/Dockerfile.dev. Named volumes for data persistence, source code volume mount with hot reload. Environment variables from .env file.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: node:20-alpine (App), postgres:15-alpine (PostgreSQL), mongo:6 (MongoDB), redis:7-alpine (Redis). Official images for platform compatibility.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing → Initial Setup Workflow
doc.snippet: Developer workflow: docker-compose up -d → Wait for health checks → docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:generate → docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:migrate → docker-compose exec app npm run prisma:seed → Access http://localhost:3000.

doc.title: Epic 10 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Container startup target: < 30 seconds (first build), < 10 seconds (subsequent starts). Hot reload: code change → server restart < 3 seconds (p95). Total resource usage: < 2.5GB RAM.

doc.title: Epic 10: Development Environment
doc.snippet: Docker Compose ile tüm servisleri başlatabilmek, local environment tek komutla ayağa kalksın. Named volumes for data persistence, health checks for dependencies, network: Default bridge network.

doc.title: Story 10.1: Development Dockerfile
doc.section: Dev Notes → Architecture Patterns and Constraints
doc.snippet: Development Dockerfile (docker/Dockerfile.dev) - Single stage, dependencies installed, source code volume mounted. Base image: node:20-alpine. Layer caching: package.json → npm ci → prisma → generate sequence.

doc.title: Story 10.1: Development Dockerfile
doc.snippet: Files to Create: docker/Dockerfile.dev, .dockerignore. Volume mount pattern: Mount source, exclude node_modules. Result: Code changes trigger NestJS watch → TypeScript recompile → Server restart.

file.reason: Base image for app service in docker-compose.yml. Build context reference. Contains node:20-alpine, npm ci, prisma generate, CMD: npm run start:dev.
NestJS watch mode command. Used as CMD in Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for hot reload.
Prisma schema for generating Prisma Client. Referenced in Dockerfile for npx prisma generate. Database connection configuration for docker-compose services.

story-context.constraints: 1. Docker Compose v2: Uses services: top-level key (compose v2 specification) 2. Dependency Management: depends_on with health checks ensures proper startup order 3. Volume Strategy: Named volumes for data persistence, bind mounts for source code 4. Network: Default bridge network enables service-to-service communication 5. Base image: node:20-alpine (Architecture standardı) 6. Port mapping: 3000 (app), 5432 (postgres), 27017 (mongodb), 6379 (redis) 7. Environment configuration: .env.example → .env.development (Story 10.3 prerequisite) 8. Multi-tenancy: Seed data domainID ile tutarlı

interface.name: Redis Service
interface.signature: image: redis:7-alpine, ports: [6379:6379], volumes: [redis_data:/data], command: redis-server --appendonly yes

tests.standards: Manual verification required for Docker Compose infrastructure. No automated tests for container orchestration. Test procedures documented in story Dev Notes section: Test 1: Docker Compose startup, Test 2: Service dependencies, Test 3: Volume persistence, Test 4: Port accessibility, Test 5: NPM scripts. Performance benchmarks: first startup < 30s, subsequent < 10s, hot reload < 3s, total < 2.5GB RAM.
tests.locations: test/ (Jest configuration), test/*.spec.ts (unit tests), test/e2e-spec.ts (e2e tests), src/**/*.spec.ts (module tests). All tests can run inside Docker container: docker-compose exec app npm test.

test.expected: All 4 services running, health checks passing, no errors, < 30s first start

test.description: App waits for postgres health check
test.expected: PostgreSQL starts first, health check passes, app starts after postgres ready, no connection errors
postgres_data, mongo_data, redis_data volumes persist, no data loss

test.command: psql -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres, mongo --host localhost --port 27017, redis-cli -h localhost -p 6379, curl http://localhost:3000
test.expected: All connections successful, services respond to queries

test.command: npm run docker:up && npm run docker:logs && npm run docker:down
test.expected: Scripts execute without errors, expected docker-compose commands run
